title: capriola ciliaris
side_effects: Capriola ciliaris can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing dizziness, nausea, and headaches in individuals who consume it in excess. Prolonged use of the plant may also lead to stomach ulcers, diarrhea, and vomiting in some cases. In rare instances, it may interact with certain medications, resulting in irregular heartbeats, low blood pressure, and respiratory problems. It's essential to use capriola ciliaris under the guidance of a healthcare professional to minimize the risk of these side effects.
